data.title: Amazon Globalstar Talks Put Spotlight On Spectrum Value And Shareholder Outcomes
data.description:
• Amazon is reported to be in advanced talks to acquire Globalstar, aiming to use its spectrum rights and satellite assets for Amazon's low Earth orbit internet network.
  • The potential deal would position Amazon to compete more directly with SpaceX's Starlink in global satellite connectivity.
  • Apple, which holds an estimated 20% economic interest in Globalstar and certain approval rights, is expected to be a key player in any transaction outcome.

Globalstar (NasdaqGS:GSAT) has drawn intense attention as its current share price of $77.73 comes alongside very strong multi year returns, including 295.6% over the past year and 384.3% over the past three years. The stock is also up 29.0% over the past week and 28.6% over the past month, reflecting how quickly expectations can reset when a potential corporate transaction emerges.

For investors watching this story, the key questions center on whether any deal materializes, how Amazon might structure an offer, and how Apple responds given its existing stake and potential veto power. Until there is confirmed news, it is worth focusing on how Globalstar's spectrum position, existing partnerships, and role in the low Earth orbit market could factor into negotiations and any future capital allocation decisions.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Globalstar by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Globalstar.

We've flagged 0 risks for Globalstar. See which could impact your investment.

This potential Amazon deal puts a clear price tag on Globalstar’s spectrum and low-Earth-orbit network, and helps explain why the share price has moved so quickly. Amazon is reportedly most interested in Globalstar’s globally cleared Band 53/n53 spectrum and existing satellite and ground infrastructure for its Amazon Leo and Project Kuiper initiatives, which are in direct competition with SpaceX’s Starlink and, to a lesser extent, OneWeb. For readers, the key point is that Globalstar offers Amazon a shortcut, with spectrum already licensed across many markets and Apple already integrated as a major customer. At the same time, the rumored US$9b deal value and Apple’s 20% economic interest create a complex negotiation triangle that could influence timing, structure, and any outcome for current shareholders.

How This Fits Into The Globalstar Narrative

  • The talks support the existing narrative that Globalstar’s spectrum and government or enterprise partnerships are meaningful assets that large players might want to control.
  • They also test the narrative’s assumption that Globalstar can independently build long-term government and IoT revenue, since full ownership by Amazon could change contract priorities and partner mix.
  • The current narrative focuses on expansion of antennas, satellites, and new modules, but does not fully account for a scenario where a single buyer acquires these assets outright.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for Globalstar to help decide what it is worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ The share price has already moved sharply on deal expectations, so any setback in negotiations with Amazon or Apple could trigger a painful reset.
  • ⚠️ Integration into a large tech group could change capital allocation priorities, potentially reducing transparency around Globalstar-specific performance and limiting future upside participation.
  • 🎁 A successful transaction could validate the economic value of Globalstar’s spectrum and infrastructure versus other satellite peers such as Starlink and OneWeb.
  • 🎁 Even if a deal does not close, the interest from Amazon highlights that Globalstar’s assets sit in a part of the sector that large technology and telecom companies are actively building into.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, focus on three things: first, any confirmation or denial of talks from Globalstar, Amazon, or Apple; second, details on how a deal might treat existing shareholders if a formal offer is announced; and third, whether Globalstar continues to sign partners or spectrum agreements while negotiations are ongoing. Clarity on Apple’s stance will be especially important, as its 20% stake and service relationship could shape both the feasibility and structure of any transaction.

data.title: Tencent Chip Deal With Huawei Reshapes AI Investment Risks And Rewards
data.description:
• Tencent Holdings (SEHK:700) has secured major orders for Huawei's new AI chips.
  • The deal aligns Tencent with other large Chinese tech groups increasing investment in domestic AI hardware.
  • The move reflects a push to build local AI infrastructure capacity amid ongoing global tech tensions.

Tencent operates across social media, gaming, cloud services and digital payments, so AI capability sits at the core of many of its platforms. The decision to source Huawei's new AI chips fits with a broader push in China to build more self contained, local technology supply chains. For investors, this chip order provides another data point on how Tencent is positioning its infrastructure for AI heavy services.

The agreement with Huawei may matter over time for Tencent's ability to train and deploy large scale AI models across its ecosystem. As Chinese tech groups allocate more capital to domestic AI hardware, readers can watch how this could influence Tencent's cost structure, product roadmap and relationships with both local and non Chinese suppliers.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Tencent Holdings by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Tencent Holdings.

📰 Beyond the headline: 0 risks and 4 things going right for Tencent Holdings that every investor should see.

This chip order ties Tencent more closely to Huawei for high-performance computing, which sits at the heart of AI-heavy services like recommendation engines, content moderation, advertising tools and gaming features. By committing to hundreds of thousands of units alongside peers such as Alibaba and ByteDance, Tencent is effectively opting into a domestically anchored supply option rather than relying solely on U.S. chipmakers. That may help support continuity for model training and inference across Tencent Cloud and consumer apps, but it also concentrates some execution risk in the success of Huawei’s AI roadmap and China’s broader semiconductor policy. For investors, the partnership sits against a backdrop where analysts have already been highlighting potential AI chip supply constraints as a risk factor. The scale of these purchases also signals that capital allocation toward AI infrastructure remains a priority, which could influence Tencent’s pace of spending and the timing of returns from AI-focused products and services.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The Tencent Holdings Narrative

  • The bulk order of Huawei AI chips lines up with the narrative that Tencent is leaning into AI across marketing, gaming, fintech and cloud to drive efficiency and new services.
  • Heavy AI infrastructure spending could pressure margins if monetization from areas like cloud GPU rentals or AI tools is slower than analysts currently factor into their expectations.
  • The narrative points to chip supply constraints as a risk, and closer Huawei reliance adds a new dimension that may not be fully captured in existing assumptions about access to advanced hardware.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for Tencent Holdings to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Greater dependence on a single domestic chip supplier could leave Tencent exposed if Huawei faces production delays or technology setbacks.
  • ⚠️ Increased AI-related capital and operating spending could weigh on profitability if AI-driven products in cloud, ads or fintech scale more slowly than expected.
  • 🎁 Aligning with Huawei on AI hardware may provide a more stable local chip pipeline at a time when access to non Chinese suppliers faces policy uncertainty.
  • 🎁 Co-investment in Huawei’s new chips alongside Alibaba and ByteDance could help Tencent stay competitive in AI capabilities versus both Chinese peers and global platforms like Alphabet and Meta.

What To Watch Going Forward

Investors can keep an eye on how quickly Tencent brings Huawei-based AI services into products such as Tencent Cloud solutions, advertising tools and gaming features, and whether management discloses any impact on capital expenditure or margins linked to these orders. It is also worth tracking commentary on chip availability, as well as Tencent’s stance on using a mix of domestic and non Chinese hardware, to gauge how flexible the AI infrastructure stack remains if regulations or export controls change. Any updates on the performance and cost profile of Huawei’s new chips compared with alternatives will also help investors judge how this decision affects Tencent’s competitiveness in AI.

data.title: Why Hyundai HT CoLtd's (KOSDAQ:039010) Soft Earnings Are Just The Beginning Of Its Problems
data.description:
Shareholders didn't appear too concerned by Hyundai HT Co,Ltd.'s (KOSDAQ:039010) weak earnings. Our analysis suggests that they may be missing some concerning details underlying the profit numbers.

This technology could replace computers: discover the 20 stocks are working to make quantum computing a reality.

Advertisement

A Closer Look At Hyundai HT CoLtd's Earnings

In high finance, the key ratio used to measure how well a company converts reported profits into free cash flow (FCF) is the accrual ratio (from cashflow). The accrual ratio subtracts the FCF from the profit for a given period, and divides the result by the average operating assets of the company over that time. You could think of the accrual ratio from cashflow as the 'non-FCF profit ratio'.

That means a negative accrual ratio is a good thing, because it shows that the company is bringing in more free cash flow than its profit would suggest. While having an accrual ratio above zero is of little concern, we do think it's worth noting when a company has a relatively high accrual ratio. That's because some academic studies have suggested that high accruals ratios tend to lead to lower profit or less profit growth.

Hyundai HT CoLtd has an accrual ratio of 0.28 for the year to December 2025. We can therefore deduce that its free cash flow fell well short of covering its statutory profit. Over the last year it actually had negative free cash flow of ₩6.5b, in contrast to the aforementioned profit of ₩10.6b. It's worth noting that Hyundai HT CoLtd generated positive FCF of ₩4.4b a year ago, so at least they've done it in the past. However, that's not all there is to consider. The accrual ratio is reflecting the impact of unusual items on statutory profit, at least in part.

Check out our latest analysis for Hyundai HT CoLtd

Note: we always recommend investors check balance sheet strength. Click here to be taken to our balance sheet analysis of Hyundai HT CoLtd.

The Impact Of Unusual Items On Profit

The fact that the company had unusual items boosting profit by ₩5.0b, in the last year, probably goes some way to explain why its accrual ratio was so weak. We can't deny that higher profits generally leave us optimistic, but we'd prefer it if the profit were to be sustainable. When we crunched the numbers on thousands of publicly listed companies, we found that a boost from unusual items in a given year is often not repeated the next year. And, after all, that's exactly what the accounting terminology implies. Hyundai HT CoLtd had a rather significant contribution from unusual items relative to its profit to December 2025. As a result, we can surmise that the unusual items are making its statutory profit significantly stronger than it would otherwise be.

Our Take On Hyundai HT CoLtd's Profit Performance

Summing up, Hyundai HT CoLtd received a nice boost to profit from unusual items, but could not match its paper profit with free cash flow. Considering all this we'd argue Hyundai HT CoLtd's profits probably give an overly generous impression of its sustainable level of profitability. If you want to do dive deeper into Hyundai HT CoLtd, you'd also look into what risks it is currently facing. For example, Hyundai HT CoLtd has 4 warning signs (and 2 which don't sit too well with us) we think you should know about.

In this article we've looked at a number of factors that can impair the utility of profit numbers, and we've come away cautious. But there are plenty of other ways to inform your opinion of a company. Examining Cashflow Against EO Technics' Earnings

One key financial ratio used to measure how well a company converts its profit to free cash flow (FCF) is the accrual ratio. Therefore, it's actually considered a good thing when a company has a negative accrual ratio, but a bad thing if its accrual ratio is positive. While having an accrual ratio above zero is of little concern, we do think it's worth noting when a company has a relatively high accrual ratio. That's because some academic studies have suggested that high accruals ratios tend to lead to lower profit or less profit growth.

Over the twelve months to December 2025, CYMECHS recorded an accrual ratio of -0.24. That indicates that its free cash flow quite significantly exceeded its statutory profit. Indeed, in the last twelve months it reported free cash flow of ₩43b, well over the ₩14.9b it reported in profit. CYMECHS shareholders are no doubt pleased that free cash flow improved over the last twelve months. Having said that, there is more to consider. We can look at how unusual items in the profit and loss statement impacted its accrual ratio, as well as explore how dilution is impacting shareholders negatively.

Check out our latest analysis for CYMECHS

Note: we always recommend investors check balance sheet strength. Click here to be taken to our balance sheet analysis of CYMECHS.

In order to understand the potential for per share returns, it is essential to consider how much a company is diluting shareholders. CYMECHS expanded the number of shares on issue by 6.9% over the last year. That means its earnings are split among a greater number of shares. To talk about net income, without noticing earnings per share, is to be distracted by the big numbers while ignoring the smaller numbers that talk to per share value. You can see a chart of CYMECHS' EPS by clicking here.

How Is Dilution Impacting CYMECHS' Earnings Per Share (EPS)?

CYMECHS has improved its profit over the last three years, with an annualized gain of 96% in that time. Net income was down 3.1% over the last twelve months. Unfortunately for shareholders, though, the earnings per share result was even worse, declining 5.5%. And so, you can see quite clearly that dilution is influencing shareholder earnings.

In the long term, if CYMECHS' earnings per share can increase, then the share price should too. But on the other hand, we'd be far less excited to learn profit (but not EPS) was improving. For the ordinary retail shareholder, EPS is a great measure to check your hypothetical "share" of the company's profit.

The Impact Of Unusual Items On Profit

CYMECHS' profit was reduced by unusual items worth ₩6.7b in the last twelve months, and this helped it produce high cash conversion, as reflected by its unusual items. This is what you'd expect to see where a company has a non-cash charge reducing paper profits. While deductions due to unusual items are disappointing in the first instance, there is a silver lining. We looked at thousands of listed companies and found that unusual items are very often one-off in nature. And, after all, that's exactly what the accounting terminology implies. If CYMECHS doesn't see those unusual expenses repeat, then all else being equal we'd expect its profit to increase over the coming year.

Our Take On CYMECHS' Profit Performance

In conclusion, both CYMECHS' accrual ratio and its unusual items suggest that its statutory earnings are probably reasonably conservative, but the dilution means that per-share performance is weaker than the statutory profit numbers imply. Based on these factors, we think CYMECHS' earnings potential is at least as good as it seems, and maybe even better! Keep in mind, when it comes to analysing a stock it's worth noting the risks involved. For example, CYMECHS has 4 warning signs (and 1 which is concerning) we think you should know about.

After our examination into the nature of CYMECHS' profit, we've come away optimistic for the company. But there is always more to discover if you are capable of focussing your mind on minutiae. data.title: Is There Now an Opportunity in NEC (TSE:6701) After Recent Share Price Volatility?
data.description:
• Wondering whether NEC is offering fair value at its current share price of ¥4,046, or if the market is missing something that you can see.
  • The stock has seen mixed recent returns, with a 1.4% gain over the last 7 days, a 3.5% decline over 30 days, a 25.5% decline year to date, and a 36.8% return over the past year, alongside a very large 3 year return and a 228.8% return over 5 years.
  • These moves have come as investors continue to digest broader newsflow around NEC's role in software and technology solutions, sector wide sentiment, and changing expectations about long term demand. Even without a single headline defining the story, the price action suggests views on risk and opportunity are still shifting.
  • NEC currently holds a valuation score of 3/6. The rest of this article will weigh that score against common valuation tools and then finish with a more complete way to think about what the shares could be worth.

NEC delivered 36.8% returns over the last year. See how this stacks up to the rest of the IT industry.

Advertisement

Approach 1: NEC Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a company could be worth by projecting its future cash flows and discounting them back to today. It essentially asks what all of NEC's future cash flows are worth in today's money.

NEC's latest twelve month free cash flow is about ¥341.2b. Analysts and extrapolated estimates point to free cash flow of ¥453.4b by the year to March 2030, with a detailed 10 year path of projections. These are discounted back using a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity model. All cash flows here are in ¥, which matches the listing currency for the shares.

When all those discounted cash flows are added up, the model suggests an intrinsic value of about ¥5,510 per share. Compared with the current share price of ¥4,046, this implies the stock is around 26.6% undervalued on this methodology.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests NEC is undervalued by 26.6%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 20 more high quality undervalued stocks.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for NEC.

Approach 2: NEC Price vs Earnings

For profitable companies like NEC, the P/E ratio is a useful shortcut because it shows how much investors are paying for each unit of current earnings. It ties the share price directly to what the business is already generating, rather than just to sales or assets.

What counts as a “normal” P/E depends on what the market expects for future growth and how risky those earnings appear. Higher expected growth and lower perceived risk usually support a higher P/E, while slower growth and higher risk tend to align with a lower P/E.

NEC currently trades on a P/E of about 21.9x. That is above the IT industry average of about 14.5x and slightly above the peer average of about 21.0x. Simply Wall St also calculates a proprietary “Fair Ratio” for NEC of about 31.1x. This is the P/E level suggested after considering earnings growth, industry, profit margins, market cap and risk factors together.

This Fair Ratio can be more informative than a simple comparison with peers or the industry because it adjusts for NEC specific drivers, rather than assuming every company deserves the same multiple. Since 31.1x is higher than the current 21.9x, this approach points to NEC trading below that Fair Ratio.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your NEC Narrative

Earlier it was mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation. Narratives step in as a simple way for you to attach a clear story about NEC to the numbers, linking your view on its future revenue, earnings and margins to a forecast and then to a fair value that can be compared with the current share price.

On Simply Wall St's Community page, Narratives let you pick or build a view that fits how you see NEC. That view then drives a live valuation that updates automatically when new information such as earnings, guidance or news on areas like BluStellar, domestic IT or international expansion is added.

For example, one NEC Narrative on the cautious side currently anchors around a fair value of ¥5,000 per share, while a more optimistic Narrative points to ¥5,450 and another to ¥6,362.5. This shows how different assumptions about long term demand, margins and P/E in the ¥3,787b to ¥4,021.3b revenue range and ¥291.3b to ¥335.2b earnings range can reasonably lead to very different views on what the shares are worth.
